skill.name: uspto-database
skill.description: Access USPTO data (Patent Search, PEDS, TSDR, assignments) when you need to query patents/trademarks and retrieve prosecution or status information programmatically.
skill.summary: Access USPTO data (Patent Search, PEDS, TSDR, assignments) when you need to query patents/trademarks...
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill provides Python clients for querying USPTO patent and trademark databases via official government API endpoints. Static analysis detected network calls to legitimate USPTO APIs and environment variable access for API authentication keys, both of which are expected for the documented purpose. All high-severity findings (weak cryptographic algorithm, shell backtick execution, system reconnaissance, dangerous obfuscation) were evaluated as false positives originating from markdown formatting and YAML descriptions. No evidence of malicious intent or data exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable access for API keys
medium_findings.description: The skill accepts and uses API keys from environment variables for authentication to USPTO services. The PatentSearchClient class accepts an api_key parameter and sends it as an X-API-KEY header in HTTP requests. SKILL.md documents USPTO_API_KEY and PATENTSVIEW_API_KEY environment variables. While expected for a USPTO API client, credential handling introduces risk if environment variables are not properly secured.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: API key handling code is present and functional, but it is standard authentication practice for government API clients with no exfiltration vector.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to external API endpoints
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs to USPTO and affiliated API endpoints across documentation and source files. All URLs point to legitimate US government services: patentsview.org, ped.uspto.gov, and tsdr.uspto.gov. These are expected for the skill's function as a patent/trademark database query tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are clearly documented USPTO API endpoints with no evidence of redirection, obfuscation, or non-official destinations.

dangerous_patterns.title: API credential sent in HTTP request header
dangerous_patterns.description: The PatentSearchClient accepts an API key and sends it as an X-API-KEY header in POST requests to patentsview.org. This is a standard API authentication pattern but introduces credential exposure risk if requests are intercepted or if the endpoint is compromised. The API key is optional (only sent when set), which reduces risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Credential-in-header pattern is standard for API auth but always carries some risk; the skill sends to a legitimate USPTO endpoint with no secondary exfiltration target.

content.user_title: Search and analyze USPTO patent and trademark data
content.value_statement: Patent researchers and IP professionals struggle to query USPTO databases efficiently using command-line tools. This skill provides documented Python clients for patent search, prosecution history retrieval, and trademark status checks through official government API endpoints.
actual_capabilities: Search US patents by keyword, inventor, assignee, or CPC classification
Retrieve patent prosecution history using the PEDS API
Check trademark status and documents through TSDR endpoints
Look up patent assignment and ownership records
Validate patent lifecycle signals for due diligence workflows
limitations: Requires API keys for USPTO and PatentsView services
Rate limits apply to USPTO API endpoints for large batch queries
Search accuracy depends on query structure and available indexed data
Trademark data retrieval is limited to documented TSDR endpoints

use_cases.title: Prior art search for patent applications
use_cases.description: Search existing US patents by keyword, inventor, and classification to find relevant prior art before filing a new patent application.

use_cases.title: Patent prosecution monitoring
use_cases.description: Track examination history, office actions, and status changes for pending patent applications using the PEDS API.
use_cases.target_user: IP portfolio manager or patent paralegal

use_cases.title: Trademark clearance research
use_cases.description: Check the current status of existing trademarks and review assignment records before registering a new mark.

prompt_templates.title: Basic patent keyword search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the USPTO patent database for patents related to renewable energy battery technology filed in the last 3 years.
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner user wants to find recent patents in a specific technology area.

prompt_templates.title: Prior art search by assignee and classification
prompt_templates.prompt: Find US patents assigned to Ford Motor Company that mention autonomous driving in their abstracts. Filter by CPC class B60W and sort by filing date.
prompt_templates.scenario: An intermediate user needs to conduct prior art analysis for a competitor.

prompt_templates.title: Prosecution history retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the full examination history for US patent application 16/123456. Summarize all office actions, applicant responses, and the current status.
prompt_templates.scenario: A patent paralegal needs to understand the prosecution timeline for a client application.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-step due diligence workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct prior art screening for a new medical device. Search by CPC class A61M, filter by filing date within the last 5 years, then retrieve prosecution history for the top 3 most relevant results to identify potential blocking patents.
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user performs a comprehensive patent landscape analysis for investment due diligence.

output_examples.input: Search for patents related to artificial intelligence in medical imaging.
output_examples.output: The system returns a list of matching patent numbers with titles, assignees, filing dates, and abstract summaries. Results can be used for prior art analysis or further prosecution history lookups.

output_examples.input: Check the prosecution history for patent application 16/123456.
output_examples.output: A timeline of all office actions, applicant responses, and status changes for this application. Key events include Non-Final Rejection (CTNF), applicant amendment, and Notice of Allowance (NOA) with their respective dates.

output_examples.input: Search for registered trademarks containing the word Eco in Class 42.
output_examples.output: A list of matching trademark records showing registration numbers, owners, filing dates, and current status such as Live, Abandoned, or Registered. Each entry includes reference codes for further TSDR detail lookups.

best_practices: Set API keys as environment variables before running any queries to keep credentials secure and configurable.
Use specific search terms combined with CPC classifications for more precise and relevant patent search results.
Verify returned patent data against official USPTO records when making critical legal or business decisions.
anti_patterns: Making a high volume of rapid requests without implementing rate limiting or delays between queries.
Relying solely on automated search results for legal decisions without human review of the actual patent documents.
Hardcoding API keys directly in scripts or configuration files instead of using environment variables.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Some USPTO API endpoints require an API key. The PatentsView API uses an optional X-API-KEY header. Check the documentation for each endpoint to understand authentication requirements.

faq.question: Is this skill affiliated with the USPTO?
faq.answer: No. This skill is a community-built tool that accesses publicly available USPTO API endpoints. It is not officially affiliated with or endorsed by the United States Patent and Trademark Office.

faq.question: Can I search for international patents with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for US patent and trademark data only. It queries USPTO APIs and does not support international patent office databases.

faq.question: How do I handle rate limits when running batch queries?
faq.answer: Add delays between requests and implement retry logic with exponential backoff. USPTO API endpoints may throttle requests that exceed their rate limits.

faq.question: What data formats do the APIs return?
faq.answer: The APIs return data in JSON format. Patent search results include structured fields for patent number, title, abstract, assignee, inventor, and classification data.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for commercial purposes?
faq.answer: The skill is released under the MIT license. However, you must comply with USPTO API terms of service and any applicable usage restrictions for the government data endpoints.
